summary:
Ukraine struck a navy base and an oil terminal in the Leningrad region, including St. Petersburg, early Thursday, hours before President Vladimir Putin's annual economic forum. President Volodymyr Zelensky confirmed the attacks, which overshadowed the conference in Russia's second-largest city. The strike hit just as Putin prepared to address business leaders, a pointed reminder that the war continues on Russian soil.

In the Persian Gulf, fresh fighting between the U.S. and Iran escalated with a drone attack closing Kuwait International Airport. The clash is the most intense in months and comes as diplomatic efforts remain stalled. Meanwhile, Israel struck a hospital in southern Lebanon, violating a U.S.-brokered partial ceasefire. Lebanese and Israeli diplomats are in Washington for a fourth round of direct talks, but Hezbollah opposes the negotiations, and pressure is mounting inside Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's government to continue the war in Lebanon.

The Trump administration proposed a 12.5% tariff on Australia, accusing it of failing to crack down on goods made with forced labor, and separate 25% tariffs on Brazil for what the U.S. calls unreasonable trade restrictions. Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva reacted with indignation, linking the decision to political rival Flávio Bolsonaro. In Ghana, parliament approved a sweeping law criminalizing LGBTQ+ identification and promotion, mandating prison sentences of three to 10 years. Community groups say people now fear losing homes, jobs, and access to healthcare.

The Allen Institute in Seattle announced it will pursue genetic therapy for brain disorders, saying scientists now understand enough about the brain to start fixing it when it breaks. The initiative aims to develop treatments for conditions like Alzheimer's. And the White House Office of Management and Budget moved to take control of billions in federal science grants, a change critics say would jeopardize the integrity of U.S. science.

headlines.title: Ukraine strikes St. Petersburg oil terminal and navy base on Putin forum day
headlines.summary: Ukraine attacked an oil terminal and navy base in the Leningrad region, including St. Petersburg, hours before Putin's annual economic forum. Zelensky confirmed the strikes, overshadowing the event.

framings.angle: Emphasized strategic timing during Putin's forum and Zelensky's confirmation
Focused on oil terminal hit and impact on Putin's economic forum

headlines.title: U.S. and Iran exchange fire in Persian Gulf, drone attack closes Kuwait airport
headlines.summary: Fresh fighting between U.S. and Iran escalated in the Persian Gulf, with a drone attack closing Kuwait International Airport. The clash is the most intense in months amid stalled diplomatic efforts.

framings.angle: Highlighted intensity of exchange and fragile ceasefire context
Reported injuries and flight diversions from Kuwait airport attack

headlines.title: Australia faces proposed 12.5% U.S. tariff over forced labor crackdown
headlines.summary: The Trump administration proposed a 12.5% tariff on Australia, accusing it of insufficiently cracking down on forced labor goods. The measure is part of broader levies on dozens of countries.

framings.angle: Focused on Australia's specific tariff threat and forced labor accusation
Contextualized as part of broader 12.5% levy on major economies

headlines.title: Allen Institute bets on genetic therapy to treat brain disorders
headlines.summary: The Allen Institute in Seattle announced it will pursue genetic therapy for brain disorders, citing sufficient scientific understanding. The initiative aims to develop treatments for conditions like Alzheimer's.

headlines.title: Drone kills seven on bus in Russian-controlled Ukraine, official says
headlines.summary: A drone strike hit a bus traveling from Moscow to Simferopol in occupied Crimea, killing seven people. The attack occurred in Russian-controlled Ukraine.

headlines.title: Israel strikes hospital in southern Lebanon despite partial ceasefire
headlines.summary: Israel hit a hospital in southern Lebanon, violating a U.S.-brokered partial truce. Diplomatic talks in Washington face opposition from Hezbollah and internal Israeli pressure.

headlines.title: Ghana passes sweeping law criminalizing LGBTQ+ activity, sparking fear
headlines.summary: Ghana's parliament approved a law criminalizing LGBTQ+ identification and promotion, with prison sentences of 3 to 10 years. Community groups fear loss of homes, jobs, and healthcare.

headlines.title: Trump administration seeks control of science funding grants
headlines.summary: The White House OMB moved to take control of billions in federal grants. Critics warn the change could jeopardize scientific integrity.

headlines.title: Trump administration proposes 25% tariffs on Brazil over trade practices
headlines.summary: The U.S. proposed 25% tariffs on Brazil, citing unreasonable trade restrictions. President Lula reacted with indignation and linked the decision to political rival Bolsonaro.
